title: The Minimalism Movement
subtitle: 为什么越来越多的人选择拥有更少

paragraphs.en: Minimalism has evolved from aesthetic preference to lifestyle philosophy embraced by millions worldwide. Adherents intentionally reduce possessions, commitments, and distractions to focus on what truly matters. This countercultural movement challenges consumer society's assumption that more stuff equals greater happiness.
paragraphs.zh: 极简主义已从审美偏好演变为被全球数百万人接受的生活哲学。追随者有意减少财物、承诺和干扰，以专注于真正重要的事物。这种反主流文化运动挑战了消费社会关于更多物品等于更大幸福的假设。

paragraphs.en: The psychological benefits of decluttering extend beyond tidy spaces. Research suggests that visual clutter increases cortisol levels and reduces focus, while simplified environments promote calm and clarity. Many minimalists report feeling liberated when they stop maintaining possessions that served no genuine purpose.
paragraphs.zh: 整理的心理好处不仅限于整洁的空间。研究表明，视觉杂乱会增加皮质醇水平并降低专注力，而简化的环境则促进平静和清晰。许多极简主义者报告说，当他们停止维护没有真正用途的物品时，感到解脱了。

paragraphs.en: Environmental considerations increasingly motivate minimalist choices. Consuming less reduces individual carbon footprints and waste generation. Some practitioners embrace minimalism specifically as an ecological response, viewing reduced consumption as more meaningful than recycling alone.
paragraphs.zh: 环境考量越来越多地驱动极简主义选择。减少消费可以减少个人碳足迹和废物产生。一些实践者专门将极简主义作为一种生态响应，认为减少消费比单独回收更有意义。

paragraphs.en: Critics note that minimalism can become its own form of consumption and privilege. Aesthetic minimalism often involves expensive, carefully curated possessions. Those with limited resources have always lived with less—not by choice but by necessity. The movement risks romanticizing poverty while remaining accessible primarily to the affluent.
paragraphs.zh: 批评者指出，极简主义可能成为其自身的消费和特权形式。审美极简主义往往涉及昂贵的、精心策划的物品。资源有限的人一直生活得很少——不是出于选择而是出于必要。这一运动有美化贫困的风险，同时主要只有富裕阶层才能接触到。

paragraphs.en: Meaningful minimalism focuses less on counting possessions than on aligning lifestyle with values. Different people require different things for fulfilling lives—minimalism is not about deprivation but about intentionality. The philosophy invites examination of what we truly need versus what marketing has convinced us to want.
paragraphs.zh: 有意义的极简主义与其说是计算物品数量，不如说是将生活方式与价值观保持一致。不同的人需要不同的东西来过充实的生活——极简主义不是关于剥夺，而是关于有意识的选择。这种哲学邀请我们审视我们真正需要什么，而不是营销让我们相信我们想要什么。
